jobs.title: How we operate
jobs.description: Stripe's operating principles describe how we work – how we make decisions, collaborate, and serve our users. They began as a way to make our implicit culture explicit, and we've refined them over time as Stripe has grown. These principles don't aim to capture everything about working here, but they express the few behaviours that matter most to building a high-trust, high-impact company. Users first. We serve millions of businesses and a meaningful fraction of global GDP. We have a weighty responsibility to those businesses and to society. We work backwards from our users' needs, indexing especially on feedback from the most innovative. Everyone at Stripe talks to users. Create with craft and beauty. With careful thought, anything can be made surprisingly great. Across every part of Stripe, we are committed to doing so. Well-crafted work indicates care for the user, and beautiful work indicates care for the world. Move with urgency and focus. We move with speed on what matters most and take the time to invest in what will make us faster tomorrow. Whereas many companies get slower until they're eventually mired in mediocrity, we aspire to become the world's fastest company. Collaborate without ego. We work as one team: no fiefdoms, no hoarding information, no "not my problem". We question assumptions, debate energetically and abandon ideas when better ones emerge. We're generous with credit and stingy with blame. We learn from mistakes together. Obsess over talent. It's every Stripe's responsibility to help hire the best and to push for excellence everywhere. Managers must relentlessly uphold our high talent bar, lead their teams with clarity and context, foster development and ultimately support Stripes in accomplishing the best work of their careers. Stay curious. Stripe is an applied exercise in learning about how businesses tick and how the world works. Since the details and possibilities are infinite, we are always seeking to learn. jobs.description: To help us process your application efficiently, please start your email subject line with [UID]. We are looking for a UI/UX Designer to join our team on a stylized anime action RPG / gacha title for PC and mobile, including controller support. Our game is story-driven with a strong combat focus, a third-person camera, and a structure built around a main lobby hub where players manage their inventory, team, and individual characters before entering level-based missions of roughly 15 minutes each. Our visual and product direction is inspired by titles such as Digimon, Wuthering Waves, Arknights: Endfield, Zenless Zone Zero and Solo Leveling: Arise. We are looking for someone who can take strong ownership over the full UI process, from early UX thinking and wireframes to polished mockups, motion concepts, and high-quality final interface assets. Responsibilities • Own the UI/UX design process across the game in close collaboration with design, programming, and art direction. • Translate gameplay requirements, feature briefs, references, and visual goals into sketches, wireframes, user flows, mockups, and final UI designs. • Help shape the UX of a cross-platform experience for PC, mobile, and console. • Create UI that is not only visually appealing, but also highly readable, intuitive, and satisfying to use. • Translate wireframes into polished interface concepts with strong motion design thinking, helping avoid static or lifeless UI presentation. • Prepare clear handoff material and documentation so engineers can implement the UI efficiently. • Contribute to a growing UI guideline / style guide document and support process documentation in parallel with production. • Collaborate where needed with monetization-related design to create strong reward presentation and other high-impact interface moments.
